serviceDescription.aws: AWS IoT SiteWise is a managed service that makes it easy to collect, store, organize, and monitor data from industrial equipment at scale to help you make better, data-driven decisions. You can use AWS IoT SiteWise to monitor operations across facilities, quickly compute common industrial performance metrics, and create applications that analyze industrial equipment data to prevent costly equipment issues and reduce gaps in production. Disclaimer: Charges for AWS IoT SiteWise are estimated based on simplified inputs provided. Monthly charges will be based on actual usage of the service.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon IoT SiteWise is a managed service that makes it easy to collect, store, organize, and monitor data from industrial equipment at scale to help you make better, data-driven decisions. You can use Amazon IoT SiteWise to monitor operations across facilities, quickly compute common industrial performance metrics, and create applications that analyze industrial equipment data to prevent costly equipment issues and reduce gaps in production. Disclaimer: Charges for Amazon IoT SiteWise are estimated based on simplified inputs provided. Monthly charges will be based on actual usage of the service.

cards.title: Ingestion Inputs
cards.helpText: To estimate the cost of ingestion, measurements, tags, cloud data availability, and percentage of latency tolerant data is required.
cards.description: The calculations below exclude free tier discounts.

components.label: Number of daily measurements
components.description: Represents total number of daily data points accumulated across all assets and sensors. For example, a single sensor, reading data every 10 seconds, generates 6 measurements per minute X 60 minutes per hour X 24 hours per day

components.label: Number of tags or sensors
components.description: Represents total number of sensors across all assets represent total number of sensors across all assets.

components.label: Cloud data availablity
components.description: The average real-time data latency from when data is generated to when its required for consumption. For example, a sensor may generate data every second but data may only be required for visualization every 10 seconds. Cloud latency would be 10.

components.label: Percentage of data that can be buffered at the edge (%)
components.description: You can buffer latency-tolerant data at the edge to optimize cost of ingestion. You can indicate percentage of data that can be buffered and buffer period in minutes.

operands.variableLabel: Percentage of data that can be buffered at the edge (%)

components.outputUnitLabel: number of measurements per tag per day

operands.variableLabel: number of measurements per tag per day
Percentage of data that can be buffered at the edge (%)

components.outputUnitLabel: KB of buffered data per day

operands.variableUnitLabel: KB of buffered data per day

components.label: Number of messages in response to egress API calls
components.description: Number of messages in response to egress API calls from the hot storage tier for operational workloads measured in 4KB increments

components.label: Number of messages in response to API calls measured in 20 KB increments
components.description: Number of messages in response to API calls measured in 20 KB increments with a 10 message minimum from a single data stream from the warm storage tier for analystical workloads.

components.label: Volume of data scanned for SQL retrieval in GB scanned
components.description: Volume of data scanned for SQL retrieval in GB scanned, metered in 100 KB increments.

operands.variableLabel: Number of messages in response to egress API calls

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] for messaging in response to egress API calls

operands.variableLabel: Number of messages in response to API calls measured in 20 KB increments

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] for messaging in response to API calls measured in 20 kb increments

operands.variableLabel: [currency] for messaging in response to egress API calls
[currency] for messaging in response to API calls measured in 20 kb increments
Volume of data scanned for SQL retrieval in GB scanned

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] for data scanned for SQL retrieval

cards.title: Computations Input
cards.helpText: Computations input represents the total number of daily computations of metrics and transforms. For example if you define one metric with the interval of 5 minutes, you will need to enter 288 (12 computations per hour X 24 hours).

components.label: Total computations of metrics and transforms

operands.variableLabel: Total computations of metrics and transforms

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] for computations of metrics and transforms

components.label: Volume of data (GB)
components.description: Total volume of data stored in the warm storage tier per month

cards.title: Export to customer owned S3 bucket input

components.label: Enable 6 hour data export to customer owned S3 bucket

components.referLabel: Enable 6 hour data export to customer owned S3 bucket

cards.title: SiteWise Assistant
cards.helpText: You can estimate the SiteWise Assistant Enablement fee and API cost for monthly API usage. Each SiteWise Assistant invocation using API or SiteWise Monitor counts as one API call.
